codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements. The worker discovered that trace_waterfall.rs was already implemented (from commit 1a3f8d2, included in 9fef3f8) and verified all 19 tests pass with cargo test, workspace build succeeds, and clippy passes with no warnings. The implementation provides: hierarchical span structure with parent-child relationships via parent_span_id and children fields, start/end timestamps per span, tool duration breakdown via ToolSpanDetails (input_bytes, output_bytes, arguments_summary) and TraceSummary (total_tool_duration_ms), and agent decision point marking via is_decision_point flag.

issues.description: input_bytes and output_bytes in ToolSpanDetails are always None with comment 'Would need to be tracked separately'. The ObservableEvent does not track input/output sizes, so this cannot be resolved without changes to the event schema. This is a known limitation documented in the code itself.

ToTraceSpan trait implementation does not detect LLM or agent-phase spans - always returns SpanKind::Tool for events with tool_invocation. This is inconsistent with TraceWaterfallBuilder::event_to_span which correctly uses is_agent_phase and is_llm_tool. The trait is a convenience extension and the main API (TraceWaterfallBuilder) handles this correctly.

summary: Feature obs-trace-waterfall passes scrutiny review. The worker correctly discovered the feature was already implemented in commit 1a3f8d2 (included in handoff commit 9fef3f8) and performed proper verification. All expected behaviors are implemented: hierarchical span structure with parent-child relationships, start/end timestamps per span, tool duration breakdown in TraceSummary, and agent decision points marked via is_decision_point flag. All 19 tests pass. Two non-blocking issues are acknowledged: input_bytes/output_bytes always being None (requires ObservableEvent schema changes), and inconsistent LLM detection between ToTraceSpan trait and main builder API. Neither blocks the feature from fulfilling its contract.
